blurb:
If you've got kids who need to burn some energy or you're looking for a spot that works for a group with different interests, Dave and Buster's gets the job done. It's a big sports bar with arcade games, so there's something for pretty much every age. Good for birthday parties, post-game celebrations, or just a Friday night when everyone wants to do something but can't agree on what.

The food is straightforward American bar food. Nothing that's going to blow you away, but it's solid enough that you're not just eating because you have to. The real draw is the arcade side, so come for the games and treat the food as a bonus.

Open until 2am on Friday and Saturday nights, so it also works as a late-night option if you're in the Cumberland area.
address: 2215 D and B Dr SE, Marietta, GA 30067, USA

blurb:
Yard House at The Battery is one of those places that works for a lot of different situations. It's a step up from a typical sports bar, with a big menu of American food and what has to be one of the longer draft beer lists in the area. Good for a Braves game day, a casual date night, or grabbing food with a group that can't agree on a type of cuisine.

The menu is broad enough that there's something for everyone, and the draft beer selection is genuinely impressive if that matters to you. It's not a cheap night out, but the quality is consistent and the atmosphere is easy.

It stays open until 12:30am on Friday and Saturday, so it also works as a late dinner option after a game.
address: 825 Battery Ave SE #320, Atlanta, GA 30339, USA

blurb:
Pappasito's is the kind of Tex-Mex spot that feels like a celebration even on a Tuesday night. It's loud, colorful, and the margaritas are the real reason most people show up. Good for a birthday dinner, a group outing, or any night when you just want something festive without a ton of effort.

The Tex-Mex classics are what people come for, and the margaritas are consistently strong. It's a chain, but it's a well-run one, and the portions are generous. Come hungry.

It can get busy on weekend evenings, so if you've got a larger group, calling ahead is a smart move.
address: 2788 Windy Hill Rd SE, Marietta, GA 30067, USA

blurb:
Derek's is one of those places you find out about from a coworker and then wonder how you went so long without knowing it existed. It's a no-frills breakfast and lunch spot on Canton Road, clean and casual, and it stays open until 5am if you're ever in a pinch for a late-night meal. Good for a quick solo lunch or grabbing food after a late shift.

The honey hot wings are what people keep coming back for, and the fish with shrimp fried rice is another crowd favorite. Everything comes out hot and made to order, which you don't always get at a spot in this price range.

Just be aware of the split hours. They close at 2:30pm and reopen at 4pm, so it's not a straight all-day operation. Plan accordingly if you're thinking about a mid-afternoon stop.
address: 1779 Canton Rd NE, Marietta, GA 30066, USA
